title: Phenomenon 106
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:⠀}}
'''BELPHEGOR'S PRIME'''
[[File:Belphe.png|center|300px|link=]] {{Underline|FFFFFF}}
Note that '''Belphegor's Prime''' is an extremely rare phenomenon, happening to most levels only once in an average of 200 years. Do not attempt to start false rumours about upcoming instances of the phenomenon in any of the affected areas, as it is unlikely that any of these will happen in one's lifetime.
{{Phenomenon Class|frequency=1|effectiveness=4|danger=5}} == [[File:Belphe.png|20px|link=]] Overview == '''Phenomenon 106''', also known as '''Belphegor's Prime''', is a rare phenomenon that may occur only in any levels labelled with prime numbers. Regardless of whether the affected level is normally deemed safe or not, any area affected by this phenomenon will instantly experience a spike in [[Phenomenon 11|instability]], as well as generating many level-wide hazardous or undesirable effects that can hinder and often harm any wanderers which happen to be in the level at the time. However, the exact nature of such manifested perils varies drastically based on the level being affected by '''Belphegor's Prime''', ranging from [[Level 37|the manifestation of multiple landmines artfully concealed beneath pools]] to [[Level 11|earthquakes seismically mighty enough to tear buildings apart]]. Additionally, the rarity of the phenomenon seems to depend on how innately safe the affected level is, with such areas as [[Level 37]] and [[Level 71]] boasting only one recorded occurrence in the past 500 years. Due to the phenomenon's innate rarity, not much is known about '''Belphegor's Prime'''. It is presumed that it is somehow intrinsically linked with [[Phenomenon 13|Casper-Bray Numbering System|Numerical Patterns]] bestowed onto levels by their respective [[Object 2|level keys]], though the exact connexion present between these two phenomena is yet to be discovered. Likewise, the reason behind this phenomenon only affecting levels numbered with a prime remains unknown; a common theory is that these levels act as fundamental building blocks to which all levels owe their existence — much like the nature of prime numbers themselves — but, with nothing to hold their own weight in the infinite abyss of [[the Void]], these prime levels are infinitely more susceptible to being destabilised, an occurrence which manifests as '''Belphegor's Prime'''. Where [[Level 0]] and [[Level 1]], numbered as well with the building blocks of mathematics which yet are not prime numbers, fit into this hypothesis is unknown, albeit some researchers believe that they may be subject to a variant of '''Belphegor's Prime''' far more severe yet far more uncommon than the regular, considering their respective numbers' sheer fundamentality. == [[File:Belphe.png|20px|link=]] Instances == Below, several documented instances of '''Belphegor's Prime''' are listed.

> FILE-002: CONSTRICTED-FLOODING
---- === Constricted Flooding === '''Constricted Flooding''' is an instance of '''Belphegor's Prime''' occurring only within [[Level 2]]. Roughly two hours before the phenomenon begins, warning signs resembling those of a pressure-build up will begin to present themselves across various areas of the level — pipes will begin to emit a high-pitched screaming sound; vents will rust at unprecedented speeds and start to drip water; pressure valves on pipelines will shatter under the immense stress build-up; and the intestinal walls of biological pipelines will swell and bulge in certain areas, giving them a bubbled and leprous appearance. Following these omens, the true horrors of '''Constricted Flooding''' will commence. All of a sudden, pipes will burst, vents will pop off, and the walls of biological pipelines will split open; and from these orifices all manner of fluids will gush out: water, [[Object 1|almond water]], [[Object 12|liquid pain]], [[Object 44|cashew water]], and various forms of digestive acids utilised in the tracts of biological pipelines. These liquids will amass and flood the tunnels of [[Level 2]] rapidly, guaranteeing anyone who has not fled the level by then an extremely low chance of survival. Records show that '''Constricted Flooding''' can continue for several days before the fluids stop flowing. Afterwards, the pipes, vents and biological pipelines will inexplicably mend themselves, and all the liquids will slowly be drained away through vents or similar orifices on the ground. The [[M.E.G.]] has calculated that, after an instance of '''Constricted Flooding''' takes place, it will be two weeks at most before wanderers can safely enter the level again. [[File:FloodedUtilityTunnel.jpg|center|250px|link=]] ----

> FILE-003: ELECTRICAL-HAZE
---- === Electrical Haze === '''Electrical Haze''' is an instance of '''Belphegor's Prime''' occurring only within [[Level 3]]. Once commenced, all machinery running on electricity within the level will begin slowly radiating their power to ionise the air, creating nothing more than a strange tingling feeling and the faint smell of ozone for those roaming the level's halls. However, due to a spike in [[Phenomenon 11|instability]], these effects are often accompanied by light passing through the ionised air being refracted in strange fashions, causing the world around any wanderer to appear distorted and warped; hence the name '''Electrical Haze'''. Once enough electrical power has been slowly transferred to the air over the course of roughly three hours, the machines will suddenly release a pulse of supercharged energy, causing an instant electric shock for any wanderers in [[Level 3]]. For those wandering the partially flooded utility tunnels beneath the main electrical station, this shock may arrive earlier, as water is an excellent electrical conductor and may greatly amplify the small amounts of electricity released in the earlier stages of '''Electrical Haze'''.Strangely, it seems that none of the machinery in [[Level 3]] is affected after an instance of '''Electrical Haze''', not even appearing to lose any power. Scientists are currently researching this seemingly unexplainable happening. [[File:Unstable3.png|center|250px|link=]] ----

> FILE-005: SHATTERED-PENTACLES
---- === Shattered Pentacles === '''Shattered Pentacles''' are an instance of '''Belphegor's Prime''' occurring only within [[Level 5]]. Unlike other examples of this phenomenon, there are no early omens or warning signs of '''Shattered Pentacles''', rendering it one of the most dangerous variants of '''Belphegor's Prime''' across the many levels of the Backrooms. When the event commences, encircled pentagrams aglow with spectral red fire will manifest on the walls, ceilings and floors of the hotel, burning for five seconds before shattering into nothingness and revealing a star-shaped hole leading only to blackness where they had once been. These fissures are connected to [[the Void]], allowing strange and enigmatic things to seep out into [[Level 5]]. These include tentacles oftentimes alluded to as appendages of [[Entity 303|the Mime]] or [[the Beast of Level 5]], clumps and conglomerates of psychic energy gathered within the Achospic PlaneA metaphysical collection of all thoughts and emotions experienced by sentient beings, composed entirely of psychic radiation and located within [[the Void]]., and even criminals sentenced to imprisonment within the [[The Void|eternal abyss]] by such authorities as the [[M.E.G.]] and the [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation|B.I.F.]] In certain cases, especially when [[Level 5]] had already experienced a sharp increase in [[Phenomenon 11|instability]] beforehand (such as during [[Phenomenon 12|the Flicker]]), some of these '''Shattered Pentacles''' have been reported to open rifts to [[the Broken]] instead. It is often theorised that [[the Beast of Level 5]] is able, with his godlike "hotelier" powers, to manipulate '''Shattered Pentacles''' to his will, thus allowing him to discreetly pull such objects as tentacles, flames and noxious gases out of [[the Void]] so as to intimidate or kill his victims. Additionally, this instance of '''Belphegor's Prime''' may actually be the cause of the Xenon Fulmination,An ancient and enigmatic catastrophic event when xenon-136 levels in the Backrooms suddenly and inexplicably skyrocketed, before beginning to fall at a far slower rate, allowing for the vague estimation of an era based on the relative amount of xenon contained within any particular object. This is a process known as xenon dating. as researchers have found xenon-136 levels to be inexplicably higher than normal within [[Level 5]]. The manifestation of '''Shattered Pentacles''' may continue for up to five hours, before it finally ceases and the hotel begins to slowly repair itself. Albeit, areas in which pentacles have fractured are likely to be unstable for the next few weeks at least, and accidentally stepping there may lead to a wanderer no-clipping into [[the Void]] or [[the Broken]]. [[File:wap-bam-boom-alakazam.jpeg|center|250px|link=]] ----

> FILE-007: NUCLEAR-SUPERSTORMS
---- === Nuclear Superstorms === '''Nuclear Superstorms'''This name is technically a misnomer, as '''Nuclear Superstorms''' do not involve anything to do with atomic nuclei, the radiation emitted from their lightning bolts instead being caused by stray positrons. are an instance of '''Belphegor's Prime''' occurring only within [[Level 7]]. Roughly seven hours before a '''Nuclear Superstorm''' begins, the first warning signs will appear: the entire level will be tinted a faint purple, a barely noticeable hue, but still there nonetheless. Afterwards, the water will begin to foam and froth anomalously; while [[the Thing on Level 7]] will become uncharacteristically inactive, rendering the ocean completely safe to swim in for a period of time. However, this sense of security does not last. Eventually, a dark storm of purple-black clouds will begin to brew above the ocean, and gales measuring up to 320km/h (roughly 200mph) will begin to blow, tossing the waves around to heights of about two miles — especially in areas of lower or altered gravity. The starting metal bunker of [[Level 7]] will often be flipped completely upside down, causing massive injury to whomever it may house at the moment. However, these effects are but the least dangerous consequences a '''Nuclear Superstorm''' may bring to the level. During a '''Nuclear Superstorm''', torrents of acidic rain will fall from the sky, capable of melting through skin in an instant and corroding many forms of metal in the matter of hours. Additionally, forks of bright purple lightning flash from the sky ever and anon; though this is no regular lightning, for its supercharged electrons are laced with positrons which anomalously emit out from it as beta particles, meaning that the area around where such a lightning bolt has struck is bound to maintain radioactive properties. Bizarrely, this radiation takes a long time to wear off, and several areas of [[Level 7]] still retain much of the radioactive qualities which befell them during the last recorded '''Nuclear Superstorm''' in 1946. A '''Nuclear Superstorm''', on average, rages for about twelve hours before the skies clear and the dust is left to settle. Once the phenomenon has concluded, the starting metal bunker of the level will inexplicably fix itself back to an upright position, losing all signs of corrosion from the damaging rain; and [[the Thing on Level 7]] will rise from wherever it has been hiding to begin its hunt once more. [[File:Purple Storm.jpg|center|350px|link=]] ----

> FILE-011: SEISMIC-TREMORS
---- === Seismic Tremors === '''Seismic Tremors''' are an instance of '''Belphegor's Prime''' occurring only within [[Level 11]]. Roughly eleven minutes before the beginning of a '''Seismic Tremor''', a faint rumbling can be heard echoing through the desolate landscape of the concrete jungle, and the ground may begin to tremble or quiver with almost negligible oscillations. However, these rumblings will eventually escalate into a full-blown earthquake, eleven times as powerful as any ever recorded on [[The Frontrooms|Earth]], capable of causing entire buildings to fall over, split in half, or even crumble from within. Massive fissures will open in the ground, opening ravines which stretch infinitely downward into what is presumably [[the Void]], though some have suggested that these cracks may lead to [[Uncharted Territory|something else entirely]]. It has been recorded that a '''Seismic Tremor''' lasts for an average of three weeks, and once it is over the cityscape will be left in ruins. Unlike other victims of '''Belphegor's Prime''', [[Level 11]] takes an incredibly long time to heal itself following the phenomenon, and though cracks in the ground are naturally sealed up over the course of mere days, the same cannot be said for the architecture, which can only anomalously rearrange itself back to its former glory in the span of several decades. It is fortunate, then, that the last recorded '''Seismic Tremor''' happened in the eighteenth century;This occurrence was chronicled by, of all people, [[Caius Taggert|Sir Caius Taggert]] of [[Sanguine Festivus]] infamy, who had been just about to exit [[Level 11]] when the earthquake began. although many are fearful that this instance of '''Belphegor's Prime''' may merely lie dormant, simply waiting to erupt. [[File:Cityabandoned.png||center|350px|link=]] ----

> FILE-013: VITREOUS-REFRACTION
---- === Vitreous Refraction === '''Vitreous Refraction''' is an instance of '''Belphegor's Prime''' occurring only within [[Level 13]]. Warning signs begin to manifest around a day before the phenomenon takes place, the most noticeable of these being the glass of the greenhouses becoming iridescent and occasionally shining far brighter than it is supposed to, sometimes causing temporary blindness in wanderers. Additionally, consumption of the grey toxin found in food within the level will begin to cause the victim to experience psychedelic colours flashing before their eyes, and a sudden wash of euphoria removing all pain from the experience. Otherwise, the toxin still operates as per normal and the wanderer will still perish in a few minutes via cessation of biological function in the lungs. After these omens have passed, the true horrors of '''Vitreous Refraction''' commence. Due to high [[Phenomenon 11|instability]] levels, all the glass panes in [[Level 13]] will begin to reflect and refract light at erratic and unpredictable angles, as well as splitting it into a multitude of different wavelengths, causing an extremely bright and unnerving light show to dance before the eyes of any wanderers who may be present within the level at the time. This event will promptly cause the aforementioned wanderers to instantly become permanently blind, though — as it only lasts for a mere thirteen seconds — it may be evaded by simply closing one's eyes for a while. As such, '''Vitreous Refraction''' is one of the less dangerous examples of '''Belphegor's Prime'''; it certainly is not lethal, yet it may cause great damage to those who are not prepared for it. [[File:Glitch-441216.jpg|center|350px|link=]] ----

> FILE-???: OTHER-EXAMPLES
---- === Other Examples ===
Note that this list is not exhaustive, and will likely be subject to updates in future. Additionally, many of these examples are derived from untested hypotheses and educated estimations, so do take the list with a grain of salt.
* '''''Shipwrecks''', [[Level 17]].'' Involves an unknown force cracking the ship's hull in half, causing the halves to drift apart from each other and sink into the murky depths. * '''''Abysmal Vortices''', [[Level 19]].'' Involves the opening of rifts leading to [[the Void]] in the level's fabric, which draw wanderers and objects into them like a black hole. Comparable to the '''Shattered Pentacles''' of [[Level 5]]. * '''''Thousand-Year Floods''', [[Level 23]].'' Involves the sudden bursting of water from the ground of the level, rising up to levels that may engulf entire churches. * '''''Morbidism''', [[Level 29]].'' Involves the transformation of all paintings in the gallery to depictions of often gruesome deaths, effectively blocking all exit routes from the level and instilling insanity into those who view the artworks for too long. * '''''Closed-Circuit Firestorms''', [[Level 31]].'' Involves the simultaneous and spontaneous combustion of every surveillance camera in the level, as well as that of the wires connecting them, resulting in a raging firestorm. * '''''Trapped Tiles''', [[Level 37]].'' Involves the manifestation of many pressure-sensitive landmines underneath certain floor tiles of the level, causing wanderers to explode if they unknowingly step on a pressured tile. * '''''Nuclear Meltdown''', [[Level 41]].'' Involves the occurrence of a reactor meltdown within any one of the already radiation-laced nuclear power plants scattered across the level, sometimes resulting in devastating explosions. * '''''Assault of the Aquatic''', [[Level 43]].'' Involves the shattering of the glass around the level as well as the protective barriers between the Offices, Laboratories and main area, allowing water, entities, animals and radiation to stream in. * '''''Rhetoric Rheumatism''', [[Level 47]]?'' Could you have known that this phenomenon involves the level being tinted red? And isn't it strange that, during this event, even those outside the level will experience joint pain when communicating any information regarding it? * '''''The Hat Man''', [[Level 53]].'' Involves a hallucinatory figure in a fedora clearer and more "real" than all others propagated by this level, who stalks the wanderer around the level, and can communicate with and even touch them, though it is thought to be harmless. * '''''Shoplifting''', [[Level 59]].'' Involves an announcement that something has been stolen from a specific shop, before dark figures manifest and begin to chase after the wanderer. It is unknown what fate awaits those who are captured. * '''''Trainwrecks''', [[Level 61]].'' Involves the sudden malfunction of the train, whether it be by inexplicable braking, the separation of train carriages, or simply the rails ceasing to generate, the latter of which results in the train tumbling into [[the Void]]. * '''''Red Skies at Morning''', [[Level 67]].'' Involves the tint of a Morning Dawn area turning from bright orange to a pale red, before the waters around that area begin to slowly turn red as well, apparently infected with a substance capable of melting through metal. * '''''Predatory Dinoflagellates''', [[Level 71]].'' Involves a manifestation of many bioluminescent, flesh-eating microörganisms within the pool water. Apparently, this phenomenon only can occur after nightfall. * '''''Shootouts''', [[Level 73]].'' Involves all firearms within the level going haywire, shooting with seemingly infinite ammunition at anywhere and everywhere, causing massive collateral damage. * '''''Radiotelegraphical Hyperstatic''', [[Level 79]].'' Involves an inexplicable amplification of the radio frequencies emitted by the Radio Field, generating a massive static effect which envelops the entire level and those who roam it. * '''''Bleeding Roses''', [[Level 83]].'' Involves the shrinking of all wanderers within the level to minuscule size so that the plants tower over them, before the roses become sentient and begin to impale wanderers with their giant gnarled thorns. * '''''The Haywire Effect''', [[Level 89]].'' Involves all uninsulated wires in the level supercharging the air eighty-nine times in quick succession, resulting in eighty-nine consecutive electric shocks for all wanderers within the level. Comparable to the '''Electrical Haze''' of [[Level 3]]. * '''''Cumulous Maelstroms''', [[Level 97]].'' Involves the formation of many vast whirlpools of clouds which pulls any nearby skyscrapers and other objects into the nebulous sea, and apparently beyond the impenetrable "seabed" as well. * '''''Ghostface''', [[Level 101]].'' The diabolical murderer of ''Scream'' infamy approaches... * '''''Molten Corruptions''', [[Level 103]].'' Involves the manifestation of many pools of "lava" hot enough to melt metal across the corrupted plain, only distinguishable from the regular neon green grid by their vivid red lines. * '''''Annihilation''', [[Level 107]].'' Involves a apparent reënactment of the [[Cascadan Conflict]], seemingly by hallucinatory figures resembling Cascadans or members of the [[The Anti-Entity Group|A.E.G.]], although it appears that wanderers and infrastructure are both somehow able to be injured or damaged during this phenomenon despite it all supposedly being a grand hallucination. * '''''Mould Infestations''', [[Level 109]].'' Involves a sudden infestation of a strange black mould capable of releasing toxic spores, which spreads across the entirety of the level and renders all of its machinery useless. * '''''Weighted Clouds''', [[Level 113]].'' Involves the sudden accumulation of anomalous matter within clouds above ground level, causing them to slowly drop like anvils and crush any wanderers who may be standing underneath them. * '''''Terminal Malfunctions''', [[Level 127]].'' Involves a terminal in the level suddenly going haywire, repeatedly rearranging its respective grid into any of the levels whose files are stored in its memory; occasionally, this may also cause the grid to collapse completely and crumble into [[the Void]]. * '''''Chromate Tempests''', [[Level 137]].'' Involves the precipitation of molten Chrome Haunting onto the fields of the level, coating all unsheltered wanderers in the substance and seemingly amplifying the Chromaunter's powers. * '''''Frequency''', [[Level 151]].'' Causes one of the many radio towers within the level to emit a loud, deafening, static sound before collapsing. This may happen to more than one tower * '''''Lawless Passages''', [[Level 157]].'' Involves the dematerialisation of all road signs within the level for a period of time, but also the opening of holes to [[the Broken]] beyond places where road signs normally would have been placed. These holes are subtle and concealed, and from a distance may appear virtually nonexistent; thus this phenomenon seems to be emphasising the importance of the stop signs' existence within the level. * '''''Twisters''', [[Level 163]].'' Involves the simultaneous formation of billions of twisters all over the level, razing farmsteads, wind turbines, "cows", and even the Old Church, and flinging them high into the air. * '''''The Great Gastronome''', [[Level 167]].'' Involves, after the wanderer is served their false meal and thanked by the level's voice, the floor opening up to reveal a fleshy maw full of teeth and ulcers, which swallows the wanderer whole. * '''''River's End''', [[Level 173]].'' Involves an extreme destabilisation of the level, which most notably includes the drying up of the never-ending river, though other factors — such as the trees falling over or clipping through the ground, or the swounds' bodies flipping inside-out — are present as well. * '''''Thrones of Thanatos''', [[Level 179]].'' Involves the sudden rushing of any chair in the vicinity of a wanderer towards them, before the wanderer's head is stuffed through the hole in one of the chairs, while the other chairs latch on to their limbs and tear the unfortunate victim apart. * '''''Lab Leaks''', [[Level 181]].'' Involves the randomised swapping of waste products into different containment locations within Lab Room 004, resulting in violent chemical reactions that are almost always fatal. * '''''Trypophobic Basements''', [[Level 193]].'' Involves the random generation of several basements across the level, which all possess extremely high levels of [[Phenomenon 11|instability]] and may result in hollows leading to [[the Void]] being formed on all surfaces of such a chamber. * '''''Alarm Bells''', [[Level 197]].'' Involves the entire hypermarket being tinted red and the sound of an alarm playing over the PA system, seemingly allowing entities to be able to track down any wanderers within the level. Comparable to the '''Shoplifting''' phenomenon of [[Level 59]]. * '''''Dry Spells''', [[Level 199]].'' Involves the drying up of water and the wilting of plants and crops in the level, including rice, grasses and trees. Also constitutes a sudden increase in temperature, creating sweltering conditions that drive wanderers to exit the level. * '''''The Wrath, [[Level 241]].''''' Creates a large thunderstorm spanning the entire level, involving tornadoes, heavy rain, floods, and an increase in false turbine populations throughout the level * '''''Eyestorms,''''' ''[[Level 293]].'' Causes every eye to detach from the wall and float through the corridors of the level. They will attempt to surround any wanderers and drive them insane. Trying to interact with these eyes will cause them to secrete a highly corrosive fluid capable of dissolving human skin on touch. This phenomenon often lasts about 2-3 hours, immediately followed by a total blackout for 5 seconds before the level returns to normal. * '''''Strikes''', [[Level 499]].'' Involves the filling of the interior of random bowling pins in the alley with various explosive substances, causing unlucky wanderers who hit one of these to accidentally explode the pins, and as a result send shrapnel directly towards them. This phenomenon does not end until all explosive pins are hit, after which the bowling alley will repair itself nigh-instantaneously. The number of pins affected is randomly picked from prime numbers in the 300s to 400s range. There is no visual indication of this event. This event overrides Level 499's phenomenon, which prevents injury. ----
== [[File:Belphe.png|20px|link=]] History == Records of '''Belphegor's Prime''' go back hundreds of years, with the first known sighting being an instance of '''Electrical Haze''' in [[Level 3]] during the mid-1400s. However, it was the Impresario Judah Casper (of [[Phenomenon 13|Casper-Bray Numbering System|Casper-Bray]] fame) who, in 1973, first connected the dots of various occurrences across prime-numbered levels — having recently co-invented the [[Phenomenon 13|Casper-Bray Numbering System]], he realised that all of these events took place on levels whose [[Object 2|level keys]] radiated a frequency correlating to a prime number. He thence catalogued the phenomenon as '''Phenomenon 1000000000000066600000000000001''', otherwise known as '''Belphegor's Prime''', a palindromic prime with the devil's number as its epicentre and an unlucky thirteen zeroes on either side. When [[the Database]] and several of the other leading groups in the Backrooms came to a mutual agreement not to create entries numbered over 500, the phenomenon's numeration was shortened to a mere '''106''' — ironically, not a prime. == [[File:Belphe.png|20px|link=]] Notes == {{Footnotes}} ---- {{Collapse|num=1|initial='''> Open Author Information'''|final='''> Close Author Information'''|body= '''Author/Wikitexter:''' [[User:The Bishop in the Blue Dimension|The Bishop]]
